servers.name: filesystem
servers.description: Read, write, and list files under a configured root. The reference MCP server for local file access; the same server Phase A's gate exercises in `experiments/rmcp_spike/`.

servers.name: git
servers.description: Inspect and manipulate the current repository — log, diff, status, blame, branch operations. Read-only by default; write operations require explicit per-tool approval.

servers.name: sqlite
servers.description: Query and update a local SQLite database. Useful when ATELIER.md points at an in-repo DB the agent should be able to inspect.

servers.name: fetch
servers.description: Fetch the contents of a URL. Counts as egress under §12 and consumes the §8 shared-state budget on every call. Use sparingly; prefer pinning specific docs via ATELIER.md.

servers.name: memory
servers.description: Persistent knowledge-graph storage across sessions. Useful when ATELIER.md alone isn't enough — long-running facts the agent should remember between sessions live here.

servers.name: github
servers.description: Read and write GitHub issues, pull requests, and repository contents. Egress per §12; counts as shared-state in §8.

requires_secrets.name: github_personal_access_token
requires_secrets.description: Personal access token with the repo scopes you intend the agent to operate against. Until OS keychain support lands, provide it through the generated environment variable only for trusted sessions.

servers.name: postgres
servers.description: Query (and conditionally update) a PostgreSQL database. The Atelier-recommended deployment uses a read-only connection string by default; write privileges should be granted via a separate registration with a separate keychain entry.

requires_secrets.name: postgres_connection_string
requires_secrets.description: Standard libpq connection string. Use a role with the minimum privilege the agent needs; until OS keychain support lands, provide it through the generated environment variable only for trusted sessions.

servers.name: puppeteer
servers.description: Launch a headless browser to fetch rendered pages, take screenshots, or interact with web UIs. Heavier than `fetch`; use when JavaScript-rendered content is unavoidable.
